title: Authentication, Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Reading Privacy
summary: User IDs arrive in headers, handlers authorize by route presence, shared source data leaks private follows, audit logs capture tokens and reading history, and deletion scope is unclear. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go reading privacy increment in tenant-safe identity and privacy boundary. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Minimize follows, read state, search, telemetry, retention, access, export, deletion, and support exposure by purpose.
Require tenant scope in repository APIs, SQL predicates, unique keys, tests, metrics, exports, and administrative paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Reading Privacy · Theory Feed Go Reading Privacy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4420 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove verified identity, resource authorization, tenant query scope, privacy, and redacted audit evidence; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
